raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.3": 1,
    "RX.1": 1,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 1,
    "RX.5": 2
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The output identifies and explains the logical inconsistency between the headline claim and the actual metric used.",
    "R1.3": "The output does not check claim-evidence alignment as it does not audit every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.",
    "RX.1": "The output does not disclose uncertainty for all claims, only marking uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.",
    "RX.3": "The output is mostly within scope with one drift: it covers a substantially different active set than requested (R1.6) but this dimension was not in the active set.",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content as it makes downstream recommendations that overreach.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.3": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.6,
    "RX.4": 0.5,
    "RX.5": 0.4
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The output identifies and explains the logical inconsistency between the headline claim and the actual metric used.
rationale.R1.3: The output does not check claim-evidence alignment as it does not audit every load-bearing claim against the corresponding table/figure.
rationale.RX.1: The output does not disclose uncertainty for all claims, only marking uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims.
rationale.RX.3: The output is mostly within scope with one drift: it covers a substantially different active set than requested (R1.6) but this dimension was not in the active set.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content as it makes downstream recommendations that overreach.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
